titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the William Henry Jackson Photographs of Arizona, Colorado, Idaho, New Mexico, Utah, and Mexico
Jackson (William Henry) photographs of Arizona, Colorado, Idaho, New Mexico, Utah, and Mexico
titlestmt.author: Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 15941253.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday August 11, 2021 at 03:43

did.unittitle: William Henry Jackson photographs of Arizona, Colorado, Idaho, New Mexico, Utah, and Mexico

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: (10 boxes)
did.abstract: Photographs taken by William Henry Jackson and published by W.H. Jackson & Co. of Arizona, Colorado, Idaho, New Mexico, Utah, and Mexico, circa 1880s.

corpname.part: W.H. Jackson Photograph and Publishing Co

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from William Reese Co. on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2015.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by number assigned in the negative.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: William Henry Jackson, Photographs of Arizona, Colorado, Idaho, New Mexico, Utah, and Mexico. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.
Collection list derived from captions in the negative.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs taken by William Henry Jackson and published by W.H. Jackson & Co. of Arizona, Colorado, Idaho, New Mexico, Utah, and Mexico, circa 1880s.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The William Henry Jackson Photographs of Arizona, Colorado, Idaho, New Mexico, Utah, and Mexico is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

bibliography.head: Bibliography
bibliography.p: William Henry Jackson (1843-1943), American photographer, opened a studio in Denver, Colorado, in 1879. He incorporated himself as W.H. Jackson Photograph and Publishing Company, Inc. in 1883.
